data_requirements.summary: File-based data model for an AI-native Python pipeline invoked via Claude Code custom skills. No relational database — all persistence is S3 objects and local JSON files. Data flows linearly: SKU Brief JSON in, brand assets checked in S3, images generated via Bedrock, text overlaid via Pillow, final packaging uploaded to S3 organized by SKU/Region/format. Pipeline run manifest (E-006) and skill invocation log (E-007) enable operational visibility without a database.

sources.name: SKU Brief
sources.volume: 1-10 KB per brief, 2-4 products per brief

sources.name: Brand Assets
sources.format: PNG/JPG images in S3 input bucket

sources.name: GenAI Image Output
sources.format: Base64-encoded PNG from Bedrock, decoded to bytes
sources.volume: 6-24 images per pipeline run (3 aspect ratios x 2-4 products; Nova Canvas primary, Titan V2 fallback)

data_requirements.storage_pattern: S3 object storage with key-based hierarchy. No database. Pipeline state is ephemeral (in-memory during run). Pipeline run manifest (E-006) written to S3 and local filesystem for operational visibility via /pipeline-status skill.
data_requirements.encryption: SSE-S3 at rest, TLS in transit

entities.name: SKU Brief
entities.description: JSON input document defining products, target region, audience, and attributes for packaging generation. Primary pipeline input — provided via /run-pipeline skill as a local file path. All string fields sanitized before prompt interpolation (prompt injection mitigation).
entities.storage: Local filesystem (JSON file path passed to /run-pipeline skill)
entities.validation: jsonschema validation at C-002. Max field lengths enforced per schema. Fail-fast before any S3 or Bedrock calls.

entities.name: Brand Asset
entities.description: Pre-existing brand assets (logos, product photos, templates) stored in the S3 input bucket. The Asset Manager (C-003) checks for these before triggering GenAI generation. S3 key convention: {brand}/{asset_type}/{filename}.
entities.storage: Amazon S3 (input_assets bucket, us-east-1, Block Public Access enabled)

entities.name: Generated Image
entities.description: Raw image output from Amazon Bedrock (Nova Canvas primary, Titan V2 fallback). One image per product-aspect-ratio combination. Ephemeral in-memory PIL bytes object during processing — never persisted as intermediate file. In dry-run mode: 1x1 pixel placeholder bytes (zero API cost). Cache: stored at ~/.cache/pai-pipeline/{sha256_hash}.png by content hash if caching enabled.
entities.storage: In-memory (bytes object, ephemeral during pipeline run). Optional local cache: ~/.cache/pai-pipeline/{sha256_hash}.png
entities.dry_run_note: dry_run=True returns 1x1 pixel placeholder PNG bytes — no Bedrock API call. No S3 upload in dry-run mode (manifest only).
entities.cache_note: Cache key: SHA-256(prompt + width + height + model_id). Cache hit skips Bedrock call entirely. Cache persists across runs for cost-efficient debug iterations.

entities.name: Packaging Output
entities.description: Final composited packaging image with Pillow text overlay (product name, attributes, regulatory info), uploaded to the S3 output bucket. S3 key convention: {sku_id}/{region}/{format}/{filename}. Not created in dry-run mode.
entities.storage: Amazon S3 (output_packaging bucket, us-east-1, Block Public Access enabled)

entities.name: Pipeline Run
entities.description: In-memory execution context for a single pipeline invocation. Accumulates metadata during the run and writes to E-006 (manifest) on completion. Correlation ID links to E-007 (skill invocation log). No longer stdout-only — structured JSON written to manifest.
entities.storage: In-memory during run → written to E-006 (manifest) on completion

fields.name: correlation_id
fields.note: Links to E-007 skill invocation log

entities.name: Pipeline Run Manifest
entities.description: Flat JSON file persisted to S3 and local filesystem after each pipeline run. Enables /pipeline-status skill to display run history without a database. One manifest per run; S3 manifest per SKU overwrites on each run (current-run view). Local manifests accumulate.
entities.storage: S3 output bucket: {sku_id}/{region}/manifest.json (overwritten per run) AND local: outputs/runs/{iso_timestamp}_{sku_id}.json (accumulates)

entities.name: Skill Invocation Log
entities.description: Structured JSON log written by Claude Code hooks (C-009) for each skill invocation. Provides debugging and audit trail for skill executions without a database. Written to local filesystem — not uploaded to S3.

fields.name: correlation_id
fields.note: Links to E-005 run_id when /run-pipeline invoked

validation_rules.summary: Input validation at C-002 (SKU Brief Parser). Pipeline fails fast on invalid input before making any API calls. String fields sanitized to prevent prompt injection.

sku_brief_validation.description: SKU brief must conform to src/schemas/sku_brief_schema.json. Required fields: brand, region, products (min 2 items). Use jsonschema library.
sku_brief_validation.enforcement: Fail-fast at C-002 before any S3 or Bedrock calls

sku_brief_validation.description: products array must contain at least 2 items (exercise requirement), each with sku_id and name
All string fields (name, flavor, attributes, regulatory_text) stripped of prompt injection patterns. Max lengths enforced.
sku_brief_validation.enforcement: C-002 sanitize_field() function before prompt interpolation in C-004

sku_brief_validation.description: region must be one of the supported values (us-west, eu, latam, apac)
sku_id: alphanumeric with optional hyphens, max 64 characters. Used in S3 key construction.

s3_key_validation.description: S3 keys from SKU brief fields sanitized — no path traversal, no special characters beyond hyphens and underscores
Uploaded files must have content_type image/png
s3_key_validation.enforcement: Pillow format detection before upload in C-007

image_validation.description: Verify Bedrock response contains base64 image data and decodes to valid PNG bytes
image_validation.enforcement: PIL Image.open() on decoded bytes in C-005 — catch exceptions, trigger retry

image_validation.description: Generated image dimensions must match requested aspect ratio (1024x1024, 576x1024, 1024x576)
image_validation.enforcement: PIL image.size check in C-005 after decode

image_validation.description: In dry-run mode, placeholder image is 1x1 pixel PNG — skip dimension check
image_validation.enforcement: C-005 dry_run branch — bypass dimension validation

data_governance.retention_policy: 90-day retention on S3 output bucket via S3 Lifecycle policy (in CloudFormation template). Input assets retained indefinitely (developer-managed). Local manifest files (outputs/runs/) not auto-expired — developer manages manually.
data_governance.encryption: SSE-S3 (AES-256) at rest for both S3 buckets. TLS 1.2+ in transit for all boto3 and anthropic[bedrock] API calls. No client-side encryption needed — no PII, internal_confidential classification.
data_governance.access_control: IAM least-privilege: single IAM role with s3:GetObject on input bucket, s3:PutObject on output bucket, bedrock:InvokeModel on Nova Canvas and Titan V2 ARNs. S3 Block Public Access enabled on both buckets. Named profile pai-exercise prevents accidental default credential use.
data_governance.data_classification: internal_confidential — brand assets and generated designs are proprietary but contain no PII
data_governance.audit: S3 access logging on output bucket. Pipeline run manifest (E-006) provides per-run audit trail. Skill invocation log (E-007) provides Claude Code session audit trail.
data_governance.ci_cd_artifacts: GitHub Actions ci.yml reads requirements.txt and src/**/*.py for lint and test. Produces test reports as GitHub Actions artifacts stored in GitHub (not S3). deploy.yml produces CloudFormation stack outputs.
data_governance.skill_invocation_flow: Claude Code Stop hook captures skill metadata and writes E-007 to outputs/logs/{date}/skill-{timestamp}.json. No S3 upload — local audit trail only.

migration_notes.summary: PoC schema is file-based and flat. No relational database is needed or planned for PoC. Production migration path moves to a metadata database for structured querying and multi-user access — see BACKLOG.md in target repo.

poc_to_production_evolution.storage: S3 + in-memory + flat JSON manifest files (E-006) + local skill logs (E-007)
poc_to_production_evolution.schema_management: SKU brief JSON schema validated at runtime via jsonschema library
poc_to_production_evolution.database: None — flat files provide adequate run history for /pipeline-status skill

poc_to_production_evolution.phase: Production v1 (BACKLOG — not in PoC scope)
poc_to_production_evolution.storage: S3 + DynamoDB or PostgreSQL on RDS for structured run history and approval workflows
changes: Promote E-006 manifest data to a DynamoDB table for queryable run history
Add structured packaging output metadata table (E-004) for search by SKU/region/status
S3 key structure unchanged — database stores metadata pointers to S3 objects
Multi-user support: RBAC for approval workflows
poc_to_production_evolution.note: PostgreSQL, DynamoDB, and RDS are BACKLOG items. Do not implement in PoC.

migration_notes.backward_compatibility: S3 key structure ({sku_id}/{region}/{format}/{filename}) is the stable contract. E-006 manifest schema will be stable. All future database implementations should read from S3 keys, not internal data structures.

_metadata.author: sa-agent
_metadata.change_log: v2.0: E-006 (Pipeline Run Manifest as flat JSON) added. E-007 (Skill Invocation Log) added. E-003 updated with dry_run flag and local cache notes. E-005 updated to write to E-006 manifest (not stdout-only), correlation_id added. Database references removed from PoC scope (moved to BACKLOG). CI/CD data flow documented. Model IDs updated to correct v2.0 values.
